source.language_note: Rulebook is Chinese; values restated in SI.

full_prompt: Design the primary landing-gear strut assembly for a CADC 2025 VTOL cargo UAV in the university class. The cargo-UAV task requires a 10 kg maximum takeoff weight (MTOW), 3 kg hover payload at 50 m altitude, and touchdown on an unprepared grass pad. The landing gear is a four-leg arrangement, and this spec covers one identical leg (the vehicle has four). Each leg is a single-part carbon-fiber sandwich strut, straight-tapered from 30 mm x 30 mm cross-section at the fuselage mount to 20 mm x 20 mm at the skid tip, overall length 400 mm, hollow with 3 mm wall thickness. The leg carries a horizontal skid tube of 400 mm length, 20 mm OD, 2 mm wall; the skid tube is bonded to the foot of the leg. The fuselage interface is four M5 bolts on a 60 mm square pattern. Leg material is CFRP quasi-isotropic laminate ([0/+45/90/-45]s) with Ex = Ey = 50 GPa, Gxy = 19 GPa, nu_xy = 0.31, density 1550 kg/m^3, in-plane tensile / compressive allowable 550 MPa, ILSS 55 MPa. Skid tube material is 7075-T6 aluminium (71.7 GPa, 0.33, 2810 kg/m^3, 503 MPa yield, 572 MPa UTS). Load cases: LC1 (Level landing) — vertical descent at 2 m/s touchdown; with MTOW 10 kg the landing load factor on 4 legs is ~4 g per leg = 98 N vertical plus horizontal drag ~25 N; apply at skid tube midpoint, fuselage interface fully fixed; LC2 (Hard landing) — vertical descent at 4 m/s; load factor ~10 g per leg = 245 N vertical, 50 N horizontal; LC3 (One-leg landing) — entire MTOW landing on one leg only, 400 N vertical, 100 N horizontal, representing asymmetric touchdown; LC4 (Drop test) — 0.5 m free fall drop test equivalent, modeled as explicit dynamic with 10 kg total mass at 3.1 m/s impact, all four legs simultaneously; LC5 (Trip-and-fall side load) — 15 g lateral at the skid tube tip, fuselage fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, LC2, LC3 (linear static), CFRP max stress < 367 MPa (allowable / 1.5), skid tube max von Mises < 335 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) max skid tube tip deflection under LC2 < 25 mm; (R3) under LC4 (explicit), peak dynamic stress < 450 MPa in CFRP and < 480 MPa in skid tube; (R4) first-mode linear buckling of leg in compression under LC3 >= 3.0; (R5) single leg assembly mass <= 200 g so four-leg total <= 800 g (within the 10 kg MTOW airframe mass budget of ~3.5 kg). Vibration modal, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly (leg + skid tube) plus a report listing per-leg mass, max stress per material per case, buckling load factor under LC3, and tip deflection under LC2.
short_prompt:
Design one of four identical landing-gear legs for a CADC 2025 university-class VTOL cargo UAV (MTOW 10 kg, 3 kg hover payload at 50 m altitude, unprepared grass pad).

Envelope: CFRP quasi-isotropic [0/+45/90/-45]s tapered strut, 30 x 30 mm at the fuselage mount tapering to 20 x 20 mm at the skid tip over 400 mm, 3 mm wall; horizontal 7075-T6 skid tube 400 mm long x 20 mm OD x 2 mm wall bonded to the foot; 4 x M5 fuselage bolts on a 60 mm square pattern.

Factor regime: FoS 1.5 on composite tensile and skid yield for linear cases; peak dynamic <= 450 MPa CFRP / 480 MPa skid in explicit; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 under one-leg landing.

Load cases.
- Level landing at 2 m/s: ~98 N vertical + 25 N horizontal at the skid midpoint per leg.
- Hard landing at 4 m/s: ~245 N vertical + 50 N horizontal per leg.
- One-leg landing: full MTOW on one leg = 400 N vertical + 100 N horizontal.
- Drop test: 0.5 m free-fall equivalent (10 kg at 3.1 m/s, all four legs simultaneously).
- Trip-and-fall side load: 15 g lateral at the skid-tube tip, fuselage fixed.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: LC4 drop-test impact requires explicit dynamics.

prompt.title: CADC 2025 VTOL cargo UAV landing gear leg
prompt.brief: Tapered CFRP landing-gear leg with aluminium skid tube for a 10 kg MTOW university VTOL cargo UAV carrying 3 kg payload.
